title: Feature Priority — Ordering and Importance Signalling
problem: Features in a tree have no explicit ordering — they render in scan/insertion order, which has no semantic meaning. Visitors can't tell which features are foundational vs incidental, and tools can't sort siblings meaningfully. Without a priority field, the tree is a flat list that buries important context.

lineage.spawnReason: The integration sprint exposed that features in the tree had no semantic ordering — priority is a first-class schema field, not a rendering concern, so it was spawned as its own story.

analysis: Priority is an integer 1–5 where 1 = highest. It controls sibling ordering in every rendering surface: the CLI lineage tree, the MCP get_lineage output, and the web app tree view. Features without a priority sort last (treated as 9999 internally). The field is optional and backward-compatible — existing features without it continue to work, they just sort by updated date. Priority is intentionally separate from completeness score: score measures how well a feature is documented; priority measures how important it is relative to its siblings.

decisions.rationale: Integers sort deterministically with no ambiguity. An enum requires mapping to a sort key anyway, adds a layer of indirection, and two features at 'high' have undefined relative order.
alternativesConsidered: Enum — cleaner to read but non-deterministic sort among same-level peers
Float 0.0–1.0 — false precision, no human-scale meaning

decisions.decision: Sort: priority ASC NULLS LAST, then updatedAt DESC
decisions.rationale: Unset features fall to the end rather than the front — opt-in semantics. Within the same priority, recency breaks ties so active work surfaces first.
alternativesConsidered: NULLS FIRST — would force all legacy features to the top, breaking existing trees

decisions.decision: Add priority to feature-schema, db schema, validators, and all rendering surfaces atomically
decisions.rationale: A half-implemented field is worse than no field — the schema, the CLI, the MCP, and the web app all need to agree on the same contract at the same time.
alternativesConsidered: Incremental rollout — creates a window where the field exists in schema but isn't used anywhere

implementation: priority: z.number().int().min(1).max(5).optional() added to FeatureSchema in both lac-cli and lifeascode feature-schema packages. DB column: smallint('priority') with idx_features_priority index. Migration: 0006_add_priority.sql. tRPC: setPriority mutation mirrors setScore; listRootFeatures and getFeatureChildren now ORDER BY priority ASC NULLS LAST, updatedAt DESC. Web app build-tree.ts: sortByPriorityThenDate replaces sortDesc. CLI lineage.ts: children.sort() by priority before rendering ASCII tree. MCP index.ts: priority shown in formatFeatureSummary as P1/5–P5/5; buildLineageTree sorts children by priority.
successCriteria: Running lac lineage feat-2026-001 shows children in priority order. The web app tree renders siblings sorted by priority. Features without priority sort last. Setting priority via the web app or MCP persists and immediately reorders the tree.
